question: 'Get __________ of here!' — completing the expression of surprise.
Read: 'The question of which languages will dominate the future is a difficult one to answer.' — The word 'dominate' means:
choices: Be the most powerful or widely used

question: She __________ to a tourist the other day and they had a very interesting conversation.
'I'm heartbroken.' — What emotion does this phrase express?
We __________ about the accident until we saw it on the news that evening.

Read: 'when students learn a foreign language, they are also learning about the culture of the country or countries where it is spoken.' — What additional thing do students learn when studying a foreign language?
choices: The culture of the country where it is spoken

question: Read: 'We are still at an early stage in the development of AI and there is no doubt it is an exciting form of technology.' — What does the writer think about AI?
choices: It is perfect and has no problems
It is exciting but needs careful control
It is only useful for adults

question: Read: 'UNIDO reports that many industries have worked together to create a plan for using AI in the country's development over the next five years.' — What has been created for Jordan's use of AI?
choices: A law banning AI in schools
A five-year development plan for using AI

question: After the argument, they __________ touch and never spoke again.
A: 'I'm extremely worried about my aunt.' B: Which is the best response?
choices: I'm so sorry to hear that.

question: She found it difficult to __________ her feelings in a simple message.
She __________ whenever anyone says something she disagrees with — very angrily.
Which sentence clearly expresses a PERSONAL OPINION rather than a fact?
choices: I feel that animal communication will be better understood in the future.
There are six million mobile phones in Jordan.
The Earth revolves around the sun.

question: Read: 'According to the website Net Positive, AI can even identify children's voices. This might mean children are directed to online advertising and content that their parents would not want.' — What is the danger of AI identifying children's voices?
choices: Children may be directed to unwanted online advertising and content
Children may be blocked from the Internet
Children may be monitored by police

question: Most teenagers __________ texting to making phone calls nowadays.
Read: 'Thanks to the work done by the naturalist Jane Goodall since the 1960s...' — 'Thanks to' means:
choices: Because of / as a result of

question: What do you think __________ this new idea? Do you like it?
How often __________ you check your messages on social media?
Will you __________ the message to Hala when you see her at school?
The rival groups had battles in which they tried to __________ each other completely.
Please __________ my message to the manager when you see him tomorrow.
Which phrase expresses that you are VERY HAPPY?
choices: It's keeping me up at night.
I've had it up to here.

question: It's hard to __________ everything when a teacher speaks very fast.
He felt __________ by her constant complaints — he couldn't take it anymore.
A good way to break the ice is to __________ the other person a sincere compliment.
He was __________ to see a snake in his garden — he stood completely still.
Read: 'we have long assumed that communication with – and between – animals must be limited.' — The word 'assumed' means:

Read: 'As a child, Jane loved reading books about a fictional character called Dr Dolittle, who could talk to animals. Perhaps this is what inspired her to go to Africa when she was in her 20s.' — What inspired Jane to go to Africa?
choices: Reading books about Dr Dolittle who could talk to animals
She saw a documentary about chimpanzees
Her parents encouraged her to go

question: By the end of this year, she __________ English for five years.
Teachers must __________ the message that hard work leads to success.
A: 'I lost my phone again.' B: '__________ ? That's the second time this week!'
A: 'I can't believe I passed!' B: Which phrase shows DISBELIEF?
choices: I'm so sorry to hear that.
That's a weight off my mind.
You've got to be kidding me!

question: Most teenagers __________ their mobile phone within reach at all times.
He has no idea how he lost his keys. He is completely __________.
Zeina likes people paying her compliments, __________?
You're coming to the party tonight, __________?
